initial_topology.method: orientation-aware normalized-affine degree on the same tetrahedral complex used by the barrier

checks.name: canonical AP-E6 continuum seed checksum is frozen

checks.detail: audits=[('uniform_ppp', True), ('uniform_ppm', True), ('uniform_pmp', True), ('uniform_pmm', True), ('layer_x', True), ('layer_xyz', True), ('five_tet_phase0', True), ('five_tet_phase1', True)]
checks.name: all six-tet and checkerboard five-tet complexes pass exact conformity, orientation, degree, and translation tests
matched p=1 has exactly constant w and decreasing R on the declared sample spacings
p=0,1,2 compact-profile slopes agree with 2-p within the declared finite-grid tolerance

checks.detail: uniform action bound => piecewise-affine H1 bound; Rellich => strong-L2 precompact subsequence; degree-sector closure is not included
checks.name: the nonnegative full action is fixed-box strongly L2 equicoercive through its base Dirichlet term
all requested fields are unanchored, stationary, admissible, and B=1 on the same complex used by their barrier
p=0 and p=2 controls both re-relax rather than being inferred by rescaling
numerical regulator-stability gate is evaluated with explicit production coverage and thresholds

barrier_gamma_audit.R_to_infinity: proved bounded-sublevel limit: fixed vacuum boundary forces the vacuum
barrier_gamma_audit.R_to_zero: proved Gamma limit: zero on L2(Omega;S3), +infinity outside; boundary trace and degree are forgotten
barrier_gamma_audit.finite_positive_R_density: open: Q_T is only the raw Cauchy-Born smooth-sampling density; a periodic multi-cell triangulation requires a homogenized cell formula
barrier_gamma_audit.positive_R_compactness: proved: inf R>0 gives affine W1,4 and C0,beta compactness for beta<1/4, hence degree closure
barrier_gamma_audit.state_space: strong L2(Omega;R4), using dual-cell piecewise-constant extensions of sphere-valued nodes and +infinity off the discrete image

fixed_box_L2_equicoercivity.scope: fixed physical box with the declared boundary data; this does not by itself preserve degree in the limit
fixed_box_L2_equicoercivity.statement: the declared base action contains the nearest-neighbour Dirichlet sum with a mesh-uniform positive coefficient; a uniform bound on the nonnegative full action therefore makes the standard piecewise-affine interpolants H1-bounded on every fixed box, and Rellich gives a strongly L2-precompact subsequence

fixed_d_classification.1<p<2: w grows and R->0: also in the necessary window, with stronger finite-a barrier
fixed_d_classification.p=0: R->0 but w->0: continuum-invisible yet not uniformly sector-coercive
fixed_d_classification.p=1: w=gamma_ref*a_ref>0 and R->0: minimal uniform-edge-margin, smooth-vanishing candidate
fixed_d_classification.p=2: w grows and R approaches a nonzero constant: a raw anisotropic Cauchy-Born quartic survives on fixed smooth samples; the periodic homogenized Gamma density remains unevaluated
fixed_d_classification.p>2: R diverges on generic nonconstant smooth fields

p_less_than_one_countersequence.construction: rotate one interior vacuum-collar vertex, with at most m incident barrier edges, so x_a=exp(-alpha/w(a)) and 0<alpha<C/(2m); the incident barrier sum stays below C while q_a-epsilon=d*x_a->0 and the rank-one local base costs vanish
p_less_than_one_countersequence.statement: if w(a)->0, uniform pair-gap coercivity is false for the barrier alone

power_law_d_generalization.necessary_joint_window: p>=1 and p+2q<2, plus epsilon>-1/3 uniformly and a^2/d->0
power_law_d_generalization.warning: these conditions control only the topology barrier; fixed-box strong-L2 equicoercivity follows separately from the base Dirichlet term, while degree-sector/topological-current compactness and the full Skyrme Gamma-liminf remain open

rigorous_pair_gap_bound.proof: write x=(q-epsilon)/d. Since b(x)=-log(x)+x-1 >= -log(x)-1 and w*b(x)<=C, x>=exp(-1-C/w)
rigorous_pair_gap_bound.statement: for every fixed C>0, if total barrier energy <= C and w>0, every regulated edge obeys q-epsilon >= d*exp(-1-C/w); C=0 is an exceptional vacuum sublevel

smooth_expansion.statement: for 1-q=O(a^2) and (1-q)/d->0, b(q)=(1-q)^2/(2d^2)+O((1-q)^3/d^3), so the three-dimensional edge sum is O(R)

numerical_gate.interpretation: even a numerical pass is evidence only; fixed-box L2 equicoercivity is proved, but degree-sector/topological-current compactness and full Skyrme Gamma-liminf/recovery remain separate obligations

smooth_profile_scaling.profile: AP-E8 C2 compact-supported degree-one hedgehog, L=6 and support radius=2
